message:
✨ acmm: scan input accepts full github.com URLs in addition to owner/repo (#8377)

Pasting 'https://github.com/ublue-os/bluefin' (or any common GitHub URL
shape) now works the same as typing 'ublue-os/bluefin' — handy when the
user is already on a repo's GitHub page and wants to scan it without
hand-extracting the slug.

Adds normalizeRepoInput() in ACMMProvider that handles:
- https://github.com/owner/repo
- https://github.com/owner/repo.git
- https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/anything
- git@github.com:owner/repo.git
- github.com/owner/repo (bare host, no protocol)

Falls through unchanged for other inputs so the downstream slug
validation still catches garbage. Applied at three entry points:

1. setRepo() in ACMMProvider — covers picker submits, badge link
   navigation, and any future programmatic callers.
2. readInitialRepo() URL-param read — so a ?repo=<github URL> in the
   address bar works too.
3. RepoPicker submit() — also reflects the cleaned slug back into the
   input so the user sees what got sent.

Placeholder updated to advertise both forms.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
